title: Sidewalk Science
subtitle: Cities are habitats too
intro: Concrete is a habitat. Plants grow through it, insects build under it, birds fly above it. Look closer.
outro: The city is alive. You just proved it.

steps.description: Find a plant growing where it's not supposed to
steps.hint: Sidewalk crack, brick wall seam, gutter -- pioneer plants are everywhere.

steps.description: Find a bug on a wall, fence, or window
steps.hint: Spiders count too -- check corners and under awnings.
Pigeons, sparrows, gulls, hawks circling -- city birds are good practice subjects.
